messages.content:
CVE-2021-41823 is a vulnerability affecting the Web Application Firewall (WAF) in Kemp LoadMaster version 7.2.54.1. This vulnerability allows certain uses of the HTML attribute 'onmouseover' to bypass the Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) protection mechanisms typically enforced by the WAF. The Common Weakness Enumeration (CWE) associated with this issue is CWE-79, which refers to improper neutralization of input before it is placed in web pages that are presented to users.

The severity of this vulnerability is rated as MEDIUM, indicating a moderate level of impact should the vulnerability be exploited successfully. Exploiting this vulnerability could potentially allow an attacker to inject malicious scripts into web pages viewed by other users, leading to unauthorized actions on behalf of users or data exfiltration.

This vulnerability specifically affects Kemp LoadMaster version 7.2.54.1, and the associated MITRE ATT&CK Technique is T1190, which involves the exploitation of public-facing applications. To mitigate the risks associated with CVE-2021-41823, organizations should implement the recommended techniques M1048, M1037, M1030, M1016, M1026, M1050, M1035, and M1051 to strengthen their security posture and protect against potential exploitation.
